name: Julekake- Cardamom Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the 2 1/2 cups all-purpose flour, yeast, and cardamom.
2. In a saucepan, heat milk, sugar, butter, and salt just until warm (115°F) and the butter is almost melted. Stir constantly.
3. Add the heated milk mixture to the flour mixture; add 1 egg. Mix on low speed of an electric mixer for half a minute.
4. Mix on high speed for three minutes, scraping sides of the bowl constantly.
5. Stir in chopped mixed candied fruits and peels (optional), raisins, and as much of the remaining 2 1/2 cups all-purpose flour as you can mix into the dough with a spoon.
6. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead in enough of the remaining all-purpose flour to make a moderately stiff dough that is smooth and elastic (about 6 to 8 minutes total).
7. Shape the dough into a ball. Place in a lightly greased bowl; turn once to grease the surface. Cover, let rise in a warm place until doubled (about 1 1/2 hours).
8. Punch down the dough; divide it in half. Cover again and let rest for 10 minutes.
9. Shape into two round loaves; place on greased baking sheets. Flatten each slightly to a 6-inch diameter. Cover and let rise until nearly doubled (45 to 60 minutes).
10. Brush loaves with egg yolk beaten with 2 tablespoons water before baking in a 350°F oven for 35 minutes or until done. Cool on a wire rack. Makes 2 round loaves.
